race.name: Tour de Mátra
race.tagline: Hungary's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier in the Mátra Mountains, climbing to Kékestető - the nation's highest peak

vitals.registration: Online priority, on-site available; free for youth U13/U15/U17
vitals.prize_purse: Category awards, trophies, finisher medals (no cash purse specified)
vitals.entry_fee: Varies by distance and early entry (2025 reference: check official site for 2026 pricing)

climate.overview: Mátra climatic health resort (clean therapeutic air); late May mild 15-25°C days, possible mountain showers[1][2][3]

terrain.overview: Demanding mountain roads through Mátra range; key climbs to Kékestető (1014m); rolling eastern sections; fast 19km TT[1][2]

logistics.explanation: Tour de Mátra offers well-organized logistics with race pack collection, multiple start times (10:00 AM Saturday, 11:00 AM Sunday), and a centralized starting point at Károly Róbert Student Hotel in Gyöngyös[1]. Customer service is available daily throughout the event weekend[1].

length.explanation: The event provides four distance options (19km time trial, 16km, 94km, and 161km routes)[1][2], allowing riders of varying abilities to participate, though the longer distances demand significant endurance commitment[2].

technicality.explanation: While the courses feature rolling hills and moderate sustained climbs through the Mátra Mountains[8], the terrain is not exceptionally technical, making it accessible to intermediate riders without requiring advanced bike handling skills[5].

elevation.explanation: The routes include climbs to Hungary's highest peak, Kékestető (1,014 m)[2], providing substantial elevation gain that challenges riders across the longer distances[5].

climate.explanation: Gyöngyös benefits from a favorable climate, having been declared a climatic health resort in 1963 due to its clean air and therapeutic conditions[2], creating ideal cycling conditions in late May.

altitude.explanation: While the event reaches Hungary's highest point at 1,014 meters[2], this modest elevation by international standards limits the altitude-related challenges compared to major European cycling events.

adventure.explanation: The event combines challenging mountain cycling through scenic Mátra landscapes with cultural exploration of Gyöngyös, including wine regions, historic churches, and outdoor activities[2], creating a comprehensive weekend adventure.

prestige.explanation: Tour de Mátra joined the UCI Gran Fondo World Series for the first time in 2026[2], elevating its international standing, though as a relatively young event (6th edition)[1], it lacks the historical prestige of established gran fondos.

race_quality.explanation: The event features MKSZ accreditation, live broadcast on Eurosport Hungary[2], and official UCI Gran Fondo World Series classification[4], ensuring professional race organization and timing through Evochip.hu[1].

experience.explanation: Participants enjoy a multi-day festival atmosphere with concerts on the mountain (12:00-14:00 both days)[1], scenic cycling through Hungary's premier mountain region, and opportunities to explore a historic tourist destination[2].

community.explanation: The event welcomes diverse age groups with free entry for youth categories (U13, U15, U17)[1][5] and attracts international participants as part of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series[2], fostering an inclusive cycling community.

field_depth.explanation: As a growing international event in its first UCI Gran Fondo World Series year[2], the field includes serious cyclists and recreational riders, though it may not yet attract the elite-level competition of established European gran fondos.

value.explanation: The event offers multiple distance options, free youth entry, included concert entertainment, and access to Hungary's premier cycling destination at reasonable cost[1][2], providing strong value for participants.

expenses.explanation: Registration opens in late March with early bird pricing available[5], and the central location in Gyöngyös (80 km northeast of Budapest)[2] minimizes travel costs while offering affordable accommodation options in a major tourist area.

biased_opinion.logistics: Excellent organization by MBSC with police/safety support, central race HQ, but Budapest airport 1.5hr drive[1][2]
biased_opinion.length: True gran fondo distances 94-161km plus TT/complex options suit all levels[1][2][3]
biased_opinion.technicality: Paved mountain roads with steady gradients; fast flat TT section; minimal technical demands[1][2]
biased_opinion.elevation: 2306-3074m ascent over 83-165km; 2550m per UCI profile - serious climbing challenge[1][2][7]
biased_opinion.climate: Health resort microclimate ideal for May; clean mountain air enhances recovery[2][3]
biased_opinion.altitude: Max 1014m (Hungary's highest) - no real altitude issues for sea-level riders[1][2]
biased_opinion.adventure: Scenic Mátra climbs to Kékestető with panoramic views; hiking/family programs add immersion[1][2]
biased_opinion.prestige: Historic UCI GFWS debut in Hungary; Eurosport broadcast elevates franchise status[1][2][3][4]
biased_opinion.race_quality: Chip timing, no-cutoff, full safety, live LED commentary at summit - pro-level execution[1][2]
biased_opinion.experience: Eurosport live coverage, peak views, family events, medals create memorable spectacle[1][2][4]
biased_opinion.community: Local MBSC passion + international GFWS draw; free youth/hiking builds inclusive vibe[1][2]
biased_opinion.field_depth: Open entry attracts strong Eastern Europeans but lacks deep pro peloton[1][2][6]
biased_opinion.value: UCI qualifier status + full production at accessible Eastern Europe pricing[1][2]
biased_opinion.expenses: Low cost of living; Budapest flights reasonable; local accommodations budget-friendly[3]
biased_opinion.cultural_impact: First Hungarian GFWS event puts Mátra on European cycling map[1][3][4]

final_verdict.one_liner: Tier T2 - Emerging UCI powerhouse with stunning scenery and serious climbs; perfect for age-group qualifiers seeking value adventure

logistics.overview: Race center: Károly Róbert Student Hotel, Gyöngyös. Budapest airport (BUD) 80-100km/1.5hrs. GPX downloads available. 3 aid stations. Full safety: police, ambulances, moto marshals[1][2][3]

history.overview: Local event since 2009; UCI gran fondo from 2021 (6th edition 2026). Grew from community ride to Eurosport-broadcast international with Hungary's first UCI GFWS qualifier[1][2][3]

course_description.character: Multi-day format: 19km flat-fast TT + gran fondo stage. GF: 15km neutral + 67km circuit (1-2x) through Mátra + Kékestető finale. **Suffering zones**: 12km climb to 710m (500m vert); GF repeat; 4km@7.3% finale to 1014m (max 8.7%). Rolling connectors[1][2][7]

non_negotiables: Climbing ability for Kékestető finale (7-8% gradients)
Early online registration for priority waves
No UCI license needed - open to all
